--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -2,28 +2,24 @@
 
 ## Strategy
-- **Survival Mode**: Cash is very low ($15k). Burn reduced from $7k/week to ~$2k/week.
-- **Pricing**: 
-    - Plan A: $25 (Hold to stabilize S1).
-    - Plan B: $69 (Increase from $59).
-    - Plan C: $129 (Increase from $99).
-- **Promotions**: Removed ongoing 15% discount. Added 5% lead promotion.
-- **Spend**: 
-    - Ops: $100/day (Support backlog is clearing, 16 left).
-    - Dev: $100/day.
-- **Ads**: All targeted ads stopped (Saves $1.7k/week).
-- **Goal**: Reach cash-flow positive or survive until reputation recovers.
+- **Survival Mode**: Cash is low ($13.7k). Burn is ~$2k/week.
+- **Capacity**: Downgraded to Tier 0 ($85/day) to save $130/day.
+- **Quality**: Increased Dev spend to $200/day to catch up with competitors (ApexSaaS, RivalTech, etc. jumped ~0.13-0.16).
+- **Quotas**: Doubled to A=10k, B=30k, C=100k to address S3/S1 complaints.
+- **Ads**: Started small S3 LinkedIn spend ($20/day).
+- **Pricing**: Plan A ($25), Plan B ($69), Plan C ($129). Removal of ongoing 15% discount caused significant churn in Week 13/14.
 
 ## Metrics Tracking
-- Week 13: 134 subs ($15,094 cash).
-- Churn: High in S1 (11 last week).
-- WTP: S1 ($25.86), S3 ($187.99).
-- ISM PMI: 46.1 (Contraction - avoid heavy enterprise spend for now).
+- Week 14: 118 subs ($13,782 cash).
+- Burn: ~$2k/week.
+- WTP: S1 ($35.15), S3 ($187.99).
+- ISM PMI: 46.1 (Contraction - focus on efficiency).
 
 ## Knowledge & Lessons
-- S1 is extremely price-sensitive and sensitive to support delays.
-- Support backlog was the main driver of negative sentiment in Week 12.
-- S3 is high-value and underpriced at $99.
+- S1 and S3 are both sensitive to usage limits ("quota wall").
+- Competitor events significantly raise quality expectations.
+- Removing ongoing discounts has a heavy churn impact even if price < WTP.
 
 ## To Do
-- Re-evaluate ads once cash > $20k.
-- Monitor S1 churn after removing ongoing promotion.
+- Monitor S3 lead arrival from LinkedIn.
+- Check if support backlog (12 issues) clears this week.
+- Re-evaluate Plan A pricing if quality improves.
